form_semantics.justification: All inputs have labels, every section uses fieldset/legend, radio groups are properly nested in their own fieldset with a legend, toggles are checkbox inputs, file input and textarea are correctly labelled, and no placeholder-only fields exist. Minor issue: the form uses aria-labelledby="account-settings-heading" but no element carries that id — the h1 is unlabelled — making it a broken ARIA reference.

element_choice.justification: Every interactive element is a native control: buttons are <button>, file upload is <input type='file'>, toggles are <input type='checkbox'>, Save Changes is <button type='submit'>, Delete Account is <button type='button'>. No div-based interactive elements anywhere.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one <h1> ('Account Settings') on the page. Section headings are provided via <legend> elements rather than redundant h2s alongside legends, which is the correct approach per the expectations. No skipped levels and no headings used decoratively.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used sparingly and correctly in most places: aria-describedby on the avatar input, tabindex="-1" on main for the skip link target. However, aria-labelledby="account-settings-heading" on the form references a non-existent id (the h1 has no matching id), which is a broken reference that provides no benefit and could mislead assistive technology.

expectations_met: Radio button groups are within a <fieldset> with a <legend> describing the group
Toggle switches use checkbox inputs (not custom div patterns)
File upload uses <input type='file'> with a label
'Delete Account' is a <button> — not a link
Section headings use consistent heading levels (legends serve as visual headings)
Legends serve as visual headings — no redundant heading + legend for same text
Radio buttons have matching name attributes within their group
The form as a whole is a <form> element
additional_observations: aria-labelledby="account-settings-heading" on <form> references a non-existent id; the <h1> has no id attribute, making this a dangling reference
Excellent use of nested <fieldset> for the notification frequency and profile visibility radio groups within their parent sections
autocomplete="nickname" on the display name field shows good attention to autofill semantics
Skip link is correctly implemented and targets main with tabindex="-1"
